title: Cave Hill 42-97862 Memorial
crew_summary: 10 killed; buried at Lisnabreeny American Cemetery
memorial_type: Repainted memorial stone on Cave Hill (listed as historic landmark by NI Environment Agency, May 2016)

external_narratives.summary: Detailed account of B-17 Flying Fortress 42-97862 crashing on Cave Hill, Belfast on 1 June 1944. The aircraft was transferring from 401st Bomb Group to 306th Bomb Group at Nutts Corner when thick fog caused disorientation. All 10 crew killed.
key_details: Diverted to Nutts Corner due to fog
All 10 buried at Lisnabreeny American Cemetery

external_narratives.summary: Local history account of the B-17 crash on Cave Hill. Notes the memorial stone was repainted and listed as a historic landmark by the NI Environment Agency in May 2016.
key_details: Memorial listed as historic landmark May 2016
Memorial located within Belfast Zoo area

external_narratives.summary: Book '42-97862' by William Lindsay and Alfred Montgomery tells the full story of the crash. Montgomery found crew member Lawrence Dundon's wedding ring at the crash site in 1991 and returned it to his widow, inspiring Richard Attenborough's film 'Closing The Ring'.
key_details: Wedding ring found at site in 1991 by Alfred Montgomery
Inspired film 'Closing The Ring' by Richard Attenborough
